content.description:
The rendering engine that turns items into DOM trees. Manages view lookup, the renderer API, and render instance tracking.

## View Resolution

When rendering an item, views are resolved via preference hierarchy:
1. Explicit view ID passed to `renderItem()`
2. Item's `preferredView` field
3. Item's type's `preferredView` field
4. Type chain lookup (walk `extends` chain, find view with matching `for_type`)
5. Fall back to [system:default-view](item://aaaaaaaa-1111-0000-0000-000000000000)

## Render Instance Registry

Tracks every rendered DOM node with indexes by:
- **Item ID**: Find all places an item is currently displayed
- **View ID**: Re-render when view code changes
- **Parent ID**: Clean up attachments when parent re-renders

Enables partial re-rendering via `rerenderItem()`, `rerenderByView()`, `rerenderByType()`.

## Renderer API

Views receive a comprehensive API object including:
- DOM creation (`createElement`)
- Item operations (`get`, `query`, `create`, `update`, `delete`)
- Rendering (`renderItem`, `rerenderItem`)
- Navigation (`navigate`, `siblingContainer`)
- View management (`getViews`, `setPreferredView`, `updateViewConfig`)
- Events (`events.on/off` - subscribe only, no emit)

## Cycle Detection

Render path is tracked; if same item appears twice in path, either calls `onCycle` callback or throws descriptive error.
